title: <div>  •  Alignments affect how the professor (game master) interacts with them.
content:
• Alignments affect how the professor (game master) interacts with them.
• Example Alignments:
• The Loyalist – Always serves their faction first.
• The Opportunist – Self-interest first, shifting sides as needed.
• The Martyr – Willing to sacrifice for others.
• The Betrayer – Gains secret bonuses when breaking alliances.

📜 Why This Works:
✔ Adds dynamic identity play—students must consider both roleplay and strategy.
✔ Factions and parties allow meaningful collaboration.
✔ Secret alignments create hidden intrigue.

📜 2. Factions & Limited Spots in Key Systems

📜 Certain in-game elements now have defined limitations.

✔ Faction Limitations
• Factions will have unlimited spots but can only take actions based on majority vote.
context: From document: RPG Character System (Fully Integrated into Weekly Modules) — chunk 463 (chunk 463/555)
